original_filename: Dolan (2025) - Users of generative AI struggle to accurately assess their own competence.pdf

decisions.reason: PsyPost news article summarizing an underlying primary study (likely Yakura et al. or similar). Not the primary source itself; the underlying paper should be ingested separately if accessible.

Re-promoted from SKIP to DEFER per user feedback (corroborating sources strengthen the KB). Re-promoted to DEFER. PsyPost article summarizes a primary study: Fernandes, Villa, Nicholls, Haavisto, Buschek, Schmidt, Kosch, Shen & Welsch — 'AI makes you smarter but none the wiser: The disconnect between performance and metacognition' in Computers in Human Behavior. ACTION ITEM: download the primary Fernandes et al. paper and ingest THAT instead of (or in addition to) this PsyPost summary. The primary covers: 2 studies including N=452 replication, AI use flattens Dunning-Kruger, AI literacy correlates with MORE overconfidence (counterintuitive), monetary incentives don't correct overestimation, AUC metacognitive sensitivity reduced. High-priority primary source for the KB's confidence-competence-gap and metacognition cluster.
